meta.title_template: MoneyWise vs {competitor} — Local-first finance compared to {competitor}
meta.description_template: Compare MoneyWise and {competitor}. Side-by-side pricing, platforms, AI, privacy, and which one fits your money.

hero.subtitle_template: Two ways to track your money. {competitor} is {one_liner}. MoneyWise is local-first, multi-currency, and brings your own AI key.

section_titles.ai_deep_dive: AI without sending your data anywhere
section_titles.privacy: Your data stays on your phone
section_titles.when_to_choose: Which one is right for you?

advantages.title: Offline-first by design
advantages.description: Every record is in an encrypted SQLite database on your phone. Works in airplane mode.

advantages.title: Bring your own AI key
advantages.description: Plug in your Anthropic or OpenAI key. Insights run with no extra subscription. Or use pooled credits in Pro.

advantages.title: Multi-currency from day one
advantages.description: Hold accounts in any currency. Convert with daily rates. No 'home currency only' lock-in.

advantages.title: No ads, no trackers
advantages.description: Nothing leaves your device unless you turn on optional encrypted sync. We literally cannot read your data.

ai_deep_dive.title: An advisor that runs on your terms
ai_deep_dive.description: Most finance apps either don't have AI, or send your transactions to a vendor's server. MoneyWise lets you choose: bring your own Anthropic or OpenAI key, or use Pro's pooled credits. Either way, the AI sees only what you ask it to see.
bullets: Keys stored in iOS Keychain — never on our servers
Daily Brief, monthly summaries, and ad-hoc Q&A
Skip AI entirely — every other feature still works

index.title: MoneyWise alternatives, side-by-side
index.subtitle: 21 finance apps compared. Pick the one that fits your money.

mint.one_liner: a free budgeting app, now discontinued
mint.positioning: Mint was once America's most popular free budgeting app — but Intuit shut it down in 2024 and migrated users to Credit Karma. If you're looking for a Mint alternative, MoneyWise offers private, local-first tracking with no ads.
mint.when_choose_competitor: Mint is no longer available for new sign-ups. If you were a Mint user, your best path is to explore Credit Karma (the official migration) or a modern alternative.
mint.when_choose_moneywise: You want a private, ad-free budgeting app that works offline, supports multiple currencies, and never sells your data. MoneyWise is the clean break that Mint never was.

faq.q: Is Mint still available in 2024?
faq.a: No. Intuit shut down Mint in early 2024 and redirected users to Credit Karma. The original Mint app and website no longer accept new sign-ups or active accounts.

faq.q: Where did Mint users go after the shutdown?
faq.a: Intuit officially migrated Mint users to Credit Karma, where transaction tracking and net worth features were added. Many users also switched to YNAB, Monarch Money, Copilot, or MoneyWise.

faq.q: Can I import my old Mint data into MoneyWise?
faq.a: MoneyWise doesn't have a one-click Mint CSV importer yet. You can export your Mint transactions as CSV from Credit Karma and manually import the most important accounts to start fresh.

faq.q: How is MoneyWise different from what Mint offered?
faq.a: Mint was free but ad-supported and stored all your data on Intuit's servers. MoneyWise is local-first — your data stays on your device in an encrypted database, with no ads and no trackers.

faq.q: Does MoneyWise have bank sync like Mint did?
faq.a: MoneyWise focuses on manual and receipt-based entry for maximum privacy. Automatic bank sync is on the roadmap. In the meantime, you can connect accounts via CSV export from your bank.

faq.q: Is MoneyWise free like Mint was?
faq.a: MoneyWise is free for unlimited records on a single device. Pro ($6.99/mo or $49.99/yr) adds multi-device sync, backup, and AI. Unlike Mint's free model, there are no ads or data brokering.

rocket-money.positioning: Rocket Money (formerly Truebill) blends budgeting with an AI-powered subscription cancellation service, bank sync, and a generous free tier. MoneyWise trades cloud automation for local-first privacy, multi-currency tracking, and BYOK AI.
rocket-money.when_choose_competitor: You have lots of unwanted subscriptions to cancel, you want a free budgeting app with automatic bank sync, and you're comfortable with an ad-supported model backed by Rocket Companies.
rocket-money.when_choose_moneywise: You want your financial data stored locally with no trackers, you travel internationally and need multi-currency support, or you want a clean AI advisor using your own key rather than a built-in black box.

faq.q: Is Rocket Money actually free?
faq.a: Rocket Money has a free tier with basic budgeting and spending tracking. Premium features — including subscription cancellation, credit monitoring, and savings automation — require the paid plan ($4–$12/mo, user-chosen).

faq.q: What is Rocket Money's AI used for?
faq.a: Rocket Money's AI primarily identifies recurring subscriptions and flags opportunities to save or cancel services. MoneyWise's AI advisor answers broader financial questions using your own Anthropic or OpenAI key.

faq.q: Does Rocket Money support multiple currencies?
faq.a: No — Rocket Money is built for US bank accounts and US dollars only. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with real-time exchange rates.

faq.q: How does Rocket Money compare to MoneyWise on privacy?
faq.a: Rocket Money is owned by Rocket Companies and earns revenue through ads, upsells, and data partnerships. MoneyWise stores your data locally with no ads and no trackers — nothing leaves your device unless you opt into encrypted cloud sync.

faq.q: Does Rocket Money have family or couple sharing?
faq.a: Rocket Money does not offer a formal family sharing plan. MoneyWise Pro supports multi-device sync, letting partners access the same accounts.

faq.q: Can I export my Rocket Money data to MoneyWise?
faq.a: You can export your transaction history as CSV from Rocket Money and import key accounts manually into MoneyWise. There is no direct automated import yet.

monarch-money.positioning: Monarch Money is a full-featured budgeting platform with strong family/couple sharing, built-in investments, and bank sync across web, iOS, and Android. MoneyWise trades cloud convenience for local-first privacy, multi-currency, and BYOK AI.
monarch-money.when_choose_competitor: You want a polished joint budgeting experience with a partner, robust investment tracking, and don't mind paying a higher subscription for a fully cloud-based service.
monarch-money.when_choose_moneywise: You want your data on your device, you handle multiple currencies, you travel frequently, or you'd rather bring your own AI key than pay for a service that reads your transactions on their servers.

faq.q: How does Monarch Money's pricing compare to MoneyWise?
faq.a: Monarch is $14.99/mo or $99.99/yr with a 7-day free trial. MoneyWise is free on a single device; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr. MoneyWise Pro costs roughly half as much annually.

faq.q: Does Monarch Money support multiple currencies?
faq.a: Monarch Money is primarily designed for US users and does not offer multi-currency account tracking. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates built in.

faq.q: Where does Monarch Money store my data?
faq.a: Monarch stores your linked bank data on their servers via Plaid and Finicity. MoneyWise keeps everything in an encrypted database on your device; cloud sync is optional and end-to-end encrypted.

faq.q: Does Monarch Money have AI features?
faq.a: Monarch Money has some AI-powered categorization and reporting. MoneyWise has an AI advisor using your own Anthropic or OpenAI key — giving you AI insights without your full transaction history being processed by a third party.

faq.q: Is Monarch Money good for couples?
faq.a: Yes — Monarch's shared budget view is one of its strongest features. MoneyWise Pro also supports multi-device sync so partners can share access, though the collaborative view is simpler than Monarch's.

faq.q: Can I import my Monarch Money data into MoneyWise?
faq.a: MoneyWise doesn't yet have a direct Monarch importer. You can export transactions as CSV from Monarch and re-enter key accounts in MoneyWise to get started.

copilot-money.one_liner: a polished iOS and Mac budgeting app
copilot-money.positioning: Copilot is a beautifully designed budgeting app built for the Apple ecosystem — bank sync, investments, and built-in AI. MoneyWise brings local-first privacy, multi-currency support, and BYOK AI to users who want more control.
copilot-money.when_choose_competitor: You live in the Apple ecosystem (iPhone + Mac), you prefer an elegant automated experience, and you're comfortable with a subscription that stores your data in the cloud.
copilot-money.when_choose_moneywise: You use Android, you travel internationally with multiple currencies, you want your data stored locally on your device, or you prefer to bring your own AI key rather than using a vendor's built-in model.

faq.q: Is Copilot available on Android?
faq.a: No — as of 2026, Copilot is iOS and macOS only. If you need Android support, MoneyWise, YNAB, and Monarch Money are cross-platform alternatives.

faq.q: How does Copilot's pricing compare to MoneyWise?
faq.a: Copilot is $7.92/mo or $95/yr, subscription-only with no free tier. MoneyWise is free for a single device with unlimited records; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr.

faq.q: Does Copilot have AI features?
faq.a: Yes, Copilot has built-in AI that analyzes your transactions. MoneyWise also has an AI advisor, but it uses your own Anthropic or OpenAI key — so your financial data never flows through a third-party AI pipeline.

faq.q: Does Copilot support multiple currencies?
faq.a: Copilot is designed primarily for US users with US bank accounts. It does not have robust multi-currency support. MoneyWise tracks accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates.

faq.q: Where does Copilot store my financial data?
faq.a: Copilot syncs your bank data through Plaid and stores it on Copilot's servers. MoneyWise stores everything on your device in an encrypted database; cloud sync is optional and encrypted end-to-end.

faq.q: Can I use both Copilot and MoneyWise?
faq.a: Yes — some users keep Copilot for its automated US bank sync and use MoneyWise for foreign currency accounts or for the offline/privacy guarantees. There's no technical conflict.

ynab.positioning: YNAB and MoneyWise both help you understand where your money goes — but they're built for different people. YNAB enforces a budget method. MoneyWise tracks your real life.
ynab.when_choose_competitor: You've already adopted zero-based budgeting and want every dollar assigned a job. Your finances are mostly in one currency, and you're happy paying for a strong methodology coach.
ynab.when_choose_moneywise: You handle multiple currencies, you want your data on your device (not in a vendor's cloud), or you want AI insights without paying for yet another subscription.

faq.q: Can I import my YNAB data into MoneyWise?
faq.a: Today, no automatic importer. You can re-create accounts and start fresh. YNAB users tend to onboard quickly because they already think in categories.

faq.q: Does MoneyWise do zero-based budgeting?
faq.a: MoneyWise has monthly category budgets with pacing, but not the strict YNAB rule-set. If the rule-set is what you love about YNAB, stay with YNAB.

faq.q: Will MoneyWise work without internet?
faq.a: Yes. Every record is on your phone in an encrypted database. The app is fully usable in airplane mode.

faq.q: How is MoneyWise's pricing different from YNAB's?
faq.a: MoneyWise is free for unlimited records on one device. Pro adds multi-device sync, backup, and pooled AI for $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr. YNAB is subscription-only at $14.99/mo or $109/yr.

faq.q: Does MoneyWise have AI like the new YNAB features?
faq.a: Yes — MoneyWise has an AI advisor. The difference: you can plug in your own Anthropic or OpenAI key, so your data isn't sent to a vendor's pipeline.

faq.q: Can I use MoneyWise alongside YNAB?
faq.a: Yes — many people start with MoneyWise for quick mobile entry and keep YNAB for the budget method. There's no lock-in either way.

empower.one_liner: a free investment dashboard and net worth tracker
empower.positioning: Empower (formerly Personal Capital) is a free financial planning platform built around investment dashboards, net worth tracking, and retirement planning. MoneyWise focuses on privacy-first, local-first budgeting with multi-currency support and BYOK AI — complementing rather than competing with Empower's investment tools.
empower.when_choose_competitor: You are primarily focused on growing and monitoring investments, retirement readiness, and net worth across brokerage and retirement accounts, and you want a free cloud-based dashboard to see it all in one place.
empower.when_choose_moneywise: You want day-to-day spending control, privacy for your transaction data, support for multiple currencies, or an AI financial advisor that uses your own API key rather than processing your data on a third-party server.

faq.q: Is Empower really free?
faq.a: Yes — Empower's financial planning tools (net worth, portfolio analysis, retirement planner, budgeting) are completely free to use. The optional Wealth Management advisory service is a paid offering starting at accounts of $100k+.

faq.q: How does Empower differ from MoneyWise?
faq.a: Empower is built around investment portfolios and retirement planning — it shines for wealth tracking. MoneyWise is built around everyday budgeting, manual entry for privacy, multi-currency accounts, and a local-first encrypted database. They serve different primary needs.

faq.q: Does Empower store my financial data on their servers?
faq.a: Yes. Empower syncs all linked accounts to their cloud servers to power its dashboards. MoneyWise stores your data locally on your device in an encrypted database, with optional end-to-end encrypted cloud sync.

faq.q: Does Empower support multiple currencies?
faq.a: Empower is designed for US-based accounts in USD. Multi-currency support is not offered. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates built in.

faq.q: Can I use both Empower and MoneyWise together?
faq.a: Many users find they complement each other: Empower for the investment and retirement big picture, MoneyWise for day-to-day budgeting with privacy and multi-currency tracking.

spendee.one_liner: a multi-currency expense tracker popular in Europe
spendee.positioning: Spendee is a freemium expense tracker with strong multi-currency support, wallet sharing, and bank sync — widely used in Europe. Both Spendee and MoneyWise support multi-currency accounts, but MoneyWise's local-first encrypted storage and BYOK AI give privacy-conscious users a unique edge.
spendee.when_choose_competitor: You want a polished visual expense tracker with wallet sharing for groups or couples, multi-currency support, and don't mind a modest monthly subscription for bank sync features.
spendee.when_choose_moneywise: You want your financial data stored locally in an encrypted database, you travel with multiple currencies and need robust tracking, or you want to use your own AI key for financial insights without your data leaving your device.

faq.q: How does Spendee pricing compare to MoneyWise?
faq.a: Spendee Premium is $5.99/mo or $35.99/yr (Plus is $1.99/mo or $14.99/yr). MoneyWise is free for a single device; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr. MoneyWise Pro includes more privacy features at a comparable price.

faq.q: Does Spendee store data locally or in the cloud?
faq.a: Spendee syncs your data to the cloud. MoneyWise stores your data locally in an encrypted database on your device, with optional end-to-end encrypted cloud sync available in the Pro plan.

faq.q: Both apps support multi-currency — what's different?
faq.a: Spendee's multi-currency support is well-regarded for group wallets and travel. MoneyWise adds local-first encryption so your multi-currency data stays private on device, plus BYOK AI for analyzing spending in any currency.

faq.q: Does Spendee have AI features?
faq.a: Spendee offers automatic categorization but no AI advisor. MoneyWise Pro includes a BYOK AI advisor using your own Anthropic or OpenAI key, keeping your data private while providing intelligent insights.

faq.q: Can Spendee sync to my bank accounts?
faq.a: Yes — bank sync is available on Spendee's Basic (limited), Plus, and Premium tiers. MoneyWise focuses on manual entry and receipt capture for maximum privacy, with bank sync on the roadmap.

faq.q: Is Spendee available on desktop?
faq.a: Spendee has a web interface; its primary experience is on iOS and Android. MoneyWise also has iOS, Android, and web support.

pocketguard.one_liner: a spending-focused budgeting app that shows how much you can safely spend
pocketguard.positioning: PocketGuard centers on one powerful idea: 'In My Pocket' — showing you exactly how much is safe to spend after bills, savings, and goals. MoneyWise offers similar spending guidance but pairs it with manual control, multi-currency support, local-first privacy, and BYOK AI for deeper financial conversations.
pocketguard.when_choose_competitor: You want a simplified, automated budgeting experience that tells you your safe-to-spend amount each day, and you're happy with a US-centric app focused purely on overspending prevention.
pocketguard.when_choose_moneywise: You want more granular control over your finances, use multiple currencies, need your data stored privately on-device, or want an AI financial advisor that uses your own API key and doesn't process your transactions on external servers.

faq.q: How does PocketGuard pricing compare to MoneyWise?
faq.a: PocketGuard Plus is $12.99/mo or $74.99/yr. MoneyWise is free for a single device; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr. MoneyWise Pro costs significantly less annually while offering more privacy features.

faq.q: What is PocketGuard's 'In My Pocket' feature?
faq.a: PocketGuard calculates your safe-to-spend amount by subtracting bills, recurring expenses, and savings goals from your account balances. It gives you a single number to guide daily spending. MoneyWise achieves similar goals through budget envelopes and AI-powered spending analysis.

faq.q: Does PocketGuard support multiple currencies?
faq.a: PocketGuard is designed for US users and does not support multiple currencies. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates built in.

faq.q: Where does PocketGuard store my financial data?
faq.a: PocketGuard syncs linked accounts to their cloud servers. MoneyWise stores all data in an encrypted database on your device, with optional end-to-end encrypted cloud sync in the Pro plan.

faq.q: Does PocketGuard have AI features?
faq.a: PocketGuard Plus includes an AI chat assistant for spending guidance. MoneyWise Pro offers a BYOK AI advisor using your own Anthropic or OpenAI key, so your full transaction history is never sent to a third party.

faq.q: Is there a web version of PocketGuard?
faq.a: PocketGuard is primarily a mobile app for iOS and Android; a full web version is not available. MoneyWise supports iOS, Android, and web.

suishouji.one_liner: China's largest personal-finance app with bank sync and voice input
suishouji.positioning: 随手记 (Feidee Money Manager) is the most widely used personal-finance app in China, made by 随手科技 (Feidee). It offers bank sync via Chinese bank partnerships, investment tracking, receipt photos, and voice input. MoneyWise brings local-first privacy, multi-currency support, and BYOK AI to users who need more than a China-centric cloud platform.
suishouji.when_choose_competitor: You are based in mainland China, want seamless sync with Chinese bank accounts, prefer a feature-rich app with a large local community, and are comfortable with a cloud-based, ad-supported freemium model.
suishouji.when_choose_moneywise: You handle multiple currencies, want your financial data stored locally on your device with no ads or trackers, need an AI advisor you fully control with your own API key, or you are outside mainland China where 随手记's bank sync does not function.

faq.q: Is 随手记 available outside mainland China?
faq.a: 随手记 can be downloaded internationally, but its bank sync feature relies on partnerships with Chinese banks and works best within mainland China. MoneyWise works globally and supports multi-currency accounts out of the box.

faq.q: Is 随手记 free to use?
faq.a: 随手记 has a free tier with ads. VIP removes ads and unlocks advanced features for approximately ¥30/month or ¥150/year (about $4.20/mo or $21/yr). MoneyWise is free on a single device with no ads; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr.

faq.q: Does 随手记 store my data locally or in the cloud?
faq.a: 随手记 syncs data to Feidee's cloud servers. MoneyWise stores everything in an encrypted database on your device. Cloud sync in MoneyWise is optional and end-to-end encrypted.

faq.q: Does 随手记 support multiple currencies?
faq.a: 随手记 is focused on CNY and does not offer robust multi-currency account tracking. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates.

faq.q: How does 随手记's AI compare to MoneyWise's?
faq.a: 随手记 does not currently offer an AI advisor. MoneyWise Pro includes a BYOK AI advisor — you plug in your own Anthropic or OpenAI key, so your transactions are never sent to a third-party AI vendor.

faq.q: Does 随手记 have voice input?
faq.a: Yes — 随手记 supports voice input for quick expense logging. MoneyWise focuses on receipt-photo capture and manual entry for a privacy-first workflow.

shayu.one_liner: a lightweight, design-forward expense tracker for the Chinese market
shayu.positioning: 鲨鱼记账 is a clean, minimalist expense tracker popular in China for its attractive design and simple user experience. It focuses on daily expense logging with budgets and receipt photos. MoneyWise offers broader platform reach, multi-currency support, local-first privacy, and BYOK AI.
shayu.when_choose_competitor: You want an extremely simple, visually clean expense tracker for everyday CNY spending, and you prefer a very low-cost annual VIP option (about ¥25/yr) on iOS or Android.
shayu.when_choose_moneywise: You handle multiple currencies, want your financial data stored locally in an encrypted database, need more than basic budgeting and receipt capture, or want an AI advisor powered by your own API key.

faq.q: Is 鲨鱼记账 available outside China?
faq.a: 鲨鱼记账 can be downloaded internationally on iOS and Android, but the app is designed for the Chinese market and CNY. MoneyWise is designed for a global audience with full multi-currency support.

faq.q: How does 鲨鱼记账 pricing compare to MoneyWise?
faq.a: 鲨鱼记账 VIP is approximately ¥25/year (about $3.50/yr) — one of the cheapest upgrade options available. MoneyWise is free on a single device; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr and includes sync, backup, and AI.

faq.q: Does 鲨鱼记账 have bank sync?
faq.a: No — 鲨鱼记账 is a manual-entry app. MoneyWise is also manual-entry focused, which keeps both apps private by default, though MoneyWise adds local encrypted storage.

faq.q: Does 鲨鱼记账 support multiple currencies?
faq.a: 鲨鱼记账 is designed for CNY and does not support multi-currency account tracking. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates.

faq.q: Does 鲨鱼记账 have AI features?
faq.a: No AI advisor is available in 鲨鱼记账. MoneyWise Pro includes a BYOK AI advisor using your own Anthropic or OpenAI key for private financial insights.

faq.q: Is there a web version of 鲨鱼记账?
faq.a: 鲨鱼记账 is mobile-only (iOS and Android). MoneyWise additionally offers a web interface alongside its mobile apps.

qianji.one_liner: a privacy-conscious, offline-first Chinese expense tracker
qianji.positioning: 钱迹 (Qianji) is a privacy-focused Chinese expense tracker that explicitly markets 'won't upload your data' (不会上传你的数据). It stores records locally by default, supports WebDAV for optional self-hosted sync, and has no ads or trackers — values it shares with MoneyWise. Where MoneyWise goes further: BYOK AI, multi-currency, and cross-platform reach beyond iOS/Android.
qianji.when_choose_competitor: If you want a fully offline, local-storage-focused Chinese expense tracker with a one-time purchase model and no subscription, 钱迹 is an excellent choice. 如果你想要一个完全离线、专注于本地存储的中文记账工具，钱迹是非常好的选择。
qianji.when_choose_moneywise: You need multi-currency support for international accounts, want an AI advisor powered by your own API key, need a web interface beyond mobile, or you are outside China and want an app designed for a global audience.

faq.q: Do 钱迹 and MoneyWise share the same privacy philosophy?
faq.a: Yes — both apps are built on the principle that your financial data should stay on your device. 钱迹 explicitly says it won't upload your data. MoneyWise stores everything in an encrypted SQLite database on-device; cloud sync is optional and end-to-end encrypted. This makes 钱迹 MoneyWise's closest sibling in the Chinese-app space.

faq.q: How does 钱迹's cloud sync work compared to MoneyWise?
faq.a: 钱迹 supports WebDAV-based sync — you can use your own cloud storage (e.g., Nextcloud) to sync data. MoneyWise Pro uses end-to-end encrypted cloud sync managed by MoneyWise, without requiring you to set up your own server.

faq.q: Does 钱迹 support multiple currencies?
faq.a: No — 钱迹 is designed for CNY and does not support multi-currency account tracking. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates, making it better suited for international users or frequent travelers.

faq.q: Does 钱迹 have AI features?
faq.a: 钱迹 does not offer an AI advisor. MoneyWise Pro includes BYOK AI — you plug in your own Anthropic or OpenAI key, so your transactions are analyzed privately without being sent to a third-party AI vendor.

faq.q: How does 钱迹 pricing compare to MoneyWise?
faq.a: 钱迹 uses a one-time in-app purchase to unlock the Pro version — no recurring subscription. MoneyWise is free on a single device; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr with sync, backup, and AI. If you prefer to avoid subscriptions entirely, 钱迹's one-time model is appealing.

faq.q: Is 钱迹 available on web or desktop?
faq.a: 钱迹 is mobile-only (iOS and Android). MoneyWise also supports a web interface, which is useful when you are at a computer and want to review your finances.

moneykeeper.positioning: MoneyKeeper (记账本) is a Chinese-developed expense tracker with a cross-border audience. It supports multi-currency tracking, budgets, and receipt photos — features that make it useful for travelers. MoneyWise goes further with local-first encrypted storage, BYOK AI, optional cloud sync, and no ads.
moneykeeper.when_choose_competitor: You want a simple, free expense tracker with multi-currency support and are comfortable with an ad-supported model or a small one-time purchase to remove ads.
moneykeeper.when_choose_moneywise: You want your financial data stored privately on-device, need BYOK AI insights, prefer no ads or trackers from the start, or want optional end-to-end encrypted cloud sync.

faq.q: Is MoneyKeeper free?
faq.a: MoneyKeeper is free with ads. A one-time in-app purchase (approximately ¥18) removes ads. MoneyWise is free on a single device with no ads at all; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr.

faq.q: Does MoneyKeeper support multiple currencies?
faq.a: Yes — MoneyKeeper includes multi-currency support, which is useful for travelers. MoneyWise also supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates and BYOK AI for multi-currency spending analysis.

faq.q: Where does MoneyKeeper store my data?
faq.a: MoneyKeeper stores data on-device but does not offer end-to-end encrypted cloud backup. MoneyWise stores everything in an encrypted database on your device; cloud sync is optional and end-to-end encrypted in Pro.

faq.q: Does MoneyKeeper have bank sync?
faq.a: No — MoneyKeeper is a manual-entry app. MoneyWise is also manual-entry focused for privacy, with bank sync on the roadmap.

faq.q: Does MoneyKeeper have AI features?
faq.a: MoneyKeeper does not offer an AI advisor. MoneyWise Pro includes a BYOK AI advisor using your own Anthropic or OpenAI key, providing private financial insights.

faq.q: How accurate is the data for MoneyKeeper?
faq.a: MoneyKeeper has limited public documentation. The data shown here is based on App Store listings and is marked low confidence. If you find inaccuracies, please let us know.

moneytree.one_liner: a privacy-focused Japanese financial data aggregator
moneytree.positioning: Moneytree (マネーツリー) is a Japanese financial data aggregator with a strong privacy positioning — no ads, no data brokering, no trackers. Like MoneyWise, Moneytree values user data privacy. The key difference: Moneytree focuses on aggregation (bank sync + investments) without budgeting tools, and is Japan-only. MoneyWise goes further with local-first encrypted storage, multi-currency support, budgeting, and BYOK AI.
moneytree.when_choose_competitor: You are based in Japan, want a no-ads, privacy-respecting aggregator for Japanese bank and investment accounts, and don't need detailed budgeting features.
moneytree.when_choose_moneywise: You want active budgeting tools alongside account aggregation, handle multiple currencies, need your data stored locally in an encrypted database, or want a BYOK AI advisor that processes your data privately on your own terms.

faq.q: Do Moneytree and MoneyWise share the same privacy philosophy?
faq.a: Both apps take privacy seriously — Moneytree explicitly avoids ads and data brokering, and MoneyWise stores all data locally in an encrypted database. The key difference: Moneytree is a cloud aggregator (your data lives on their servers), while MoneyWise keeps everything on-device unless you opt into encrypted sync.

faq.q: Does Moneytree support budgeting?
faq.a: Moneytree focuses on financial data aggregation rather than active budgeting. MoneyWise has monthly category budgets with spending pace tracking.

faq.q: Does Moneytree support multiple currencies?
faq.a: Moneytree is designed for the Japanese market and JPY. Multi-currency support is not offered. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates.

faq.q: Is Moneytree available outside Japan?
faq.a: Moneytree's bank sync relies on Japanese financial institution partnerships and works best within Japan. MoneyWise works globally and supports multi-currency accounts out of the box.

faq.q: Does Moneytree have AI features?
faq.a: Moneytree does not currently offer an AI advisor. MoneyWise Pro includes a BYOK AI advisor — you plug in your own Anthropic or OpenAI key, so your transactions are never sent to a third-party AI vendor.

faq.q: How does Moneytree pricing compare to MoneyWise?
faq.a: Moneytree has a free tier; Moneytree Plus is approximately ¥500/month (about $3.30/mo). MoneyWise is free on a single device; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr with multi-device sync, backup, budgeting, and BYOK AI.

money-forward-me.one_liner: Japan's largest personal-finance aggregator with 2,500+ institution connections
money-forward-me.positioning: Money Forward ME (マネーフォワード ME) is the dominant personal-finance aggregator in Japan, connecting to 2,500+ financial institutions — banks, credit cards, brokerage accounts, and more. MoneyWise brings local-first privacy, multi-currency tracking, and BYOK AI to users who need more than a Japan-centric cloud aggregation platform.
money-forward-me.when_choose_competitor: You are based in Japan, want automatic aggregation of all your Japanese bank accounts, credit cards, and investment portfolios in one place, and are comfortable with a cloud-based subscription service.
money-forward-me.when_choose_moneywise: You handle multiple currencies, want your financial data stored locally on your device with no ads or trackers, need an AI advisor you fully control with your own API key, or you are outside Japan where Money Forward ME's institution sync does not function.

faq.q: Is Money Forward ME available outside Japan?
faq.a: Money Forward ME can be downloaded internationally, but its core feature — connecting to 2,500+ Japanese financial institutions — only works within Japan. MoneyWise works globally and supports multi-currency accounts out of the box.

faq.q: How does Money Forward ME pricing work?
faq.a: Money Forward ME has a free tier with limitations (e.g., limited account connections). Premium is ¥500/month via web or ¥720/month via App Store/Play (about $4.80/mo). An annual plan is also available. MoneyWise is free on a single device; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr.

faq.q: Does Money Forward ME store my data locally or in the cloud?
faq.a: Money Forward ME syncs all linked account data to its cloud servers. MoneyWise stores everything in an encrypted database on your device. Cloud sync in MoneyWise is optional and end-to-end encrypted.

faq.q: Does Money Forward ME support multiple currencies?
faq.a: Money Forward ME is focused on the Japanese market and JPY. It does not offer multi-currency account tracking. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates.

faq.q: Does Money Forward ME have AI features?
faq.a: Money Forward ME does not currently offer an AI advisor. MoneyWise Pro includes a BYOK AI advisor — you plug in your own Anthropic or OpenAI key, so your transactions are never sent to a third-party AI vendor.

faq.q: Does Money Forward ME support investment tracking?
faq.a: Yes — one of Money Forward ME's key strengths is aggregating brokerage and investment accounts alongside bank and card data. MoneyWise does not currently aggregate brokerage accounts, but provides strong expense and budget tracking.

zaim.one_liner: Japan's most popular household-finance app (家計簿)
zaim.positioning: Zaim is the leading 家計簿 (household finance) app in Japan, with Japanese bank sync, receipt scanning, and budgeting built for domestic users. MoneyWise brings local-first privacy, multi-currency tracking, and BYOK AI to users who need more than a Japan-centric cloud platform.
zaim.when_choose_competitor: You are based in Japan, want seamless sync with Japanese bank accounts and credit cards, prefer a mature app with a large local community, and are comfortable with a cloud-based freemium model.
zaim.when_choose_moneywise: You handle multiple currencies, want your financial data stored locally on your device with no ads or trackers, need an AI advisor you fully control with your own API key, or you are outside Japan where Zaim's bank sync does not function.

faq.q: Is Zaim available outside Japan?
faq.a: Zaim can be downloaded internationally, but its bank sync relies on Japanese financial institution partnerships and works best within Japan. MoneyWise works globally and supports multi-currency accounts out of the box.

faq.q: Is Zaim free to use?
faq.a: Zaim has a free tier with ads. Premium removes ads and unlocks advanced features for approximately ¥480/month or ¥4,800/year (about $3.30/mo or $32/yr). MoneyWise is free on a single device with no ads; Pro is $6.99/mo or $49.99/yr.

faq.q: Does Zaim store my data locally or in the cloud?
faq.a: Zaim syncs data to its cloud servers. MoneyWise stores everything in an encrypted database on your device. Cloud sync in MoneyWise is optional and end-to-end encrypted.

faq.q: Does Zaim support multiple currencies?
faq.a: Zaim is focused on JPY and does not offer robust multi-currency account tracking. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates.

faq.q: Does Zaim have AI features?
faq.a: Zaim does not currently offer an AI advisor. MoneyWise Pro includes a BYOK AI advisor — you plug in your own Anthropic or OpenAI key, so your transactions are never sent to a third-party AI vendor.

faq.q: Does Zaim have receipt scanning?
faq.a: Yes — Zaim supports receipt photo capture for quick expense logging. MoneyWise also supports receipt photos for a privacy-first workflow.

yimu.one_liner: a cute illustrated expense tracker popular with younger Chinese users
yimu.positioning: 一木记账 stands out for its sticker-style illustrations and friendly, approachable design — popular among younger Chinese users who want expense tracking to feel less like a chore. It offers budgets, receipt photos, and optional cloud sync. MoneyWise complements with local-first privacy, multi-currency support, and BYOK AI.

yimu.when_choose_moneywise: You need multi-currency accounts, want your data stored privately on-device in an encrypted database, want an AI advisor powered by your own API key, or need a web interface alongside your mobile app.

faq.a: 一木记账 is known for its illustrated sticker style and approachable design, which appeals to younger users who find traditional finance apps intimidating. MoneyWise offers a clean, professional interface focused on privacy and multi-currency tracking.

faq.q: How does 一木记账 pricing compare to MoneyWise?

faq.q: Does 一木记账 support multiple currencies?
faq.a: No — 一木记账 is designed for CNY and does not offer multi-currency account tracking. MoneyWise supports accounts in any currency with daily exchange rates built in.

faq.q: Is 一木记账 available outside China?
